examples.title: Aluminum Bases
examples.description:
Machining two long aluminum bases simultaneously on a modular fixture
plate. The red volumes in the preview are the raw stock removed by
the program — typical demonstration of a high-MRR roughing pass
followed by finishing toolpaths.

TODO: edit this description before publishing.

examples.title: Aluminum Base Plate
examples.description:
Single-sided machining of an aluminum base plate with two large circular
pockets and counterbore features. Demonstrates a basic but complete CAM
workflow: face, contour, pocket, bore, drill — produced from one work
coordinate system without flipping the part.

TODO: edit this description before publishing.

examples.title: Holder 01
examples.description:
Multi-cavity holder machined from a single aluminum plate — rows of
rectangular pockets for batch fixturing of small parts. The whole sheet
is hogged out in one setup on the AP4060 with a roughing + finishing
pass for each pocket row.

TODO: edit this description before publishing.

examples.title: Modular Vise
examples.description:
Step 1 of machining a modular CNC vise body. Workpieces are clamped on a
custom fixture plate with a regular pattern of T-slots — multiple parts
are roughed and finished in a single setup to minimize handling time.

TODO: edit this description before publishing.

examples.title: Plastic Plates
examples.description:
Batch machining of 6 plastic plates in one setup. Demonstrates an
efficient fixturing layout where the parts share a single zero point
and toolpath dataset — every operation processes all 6 instances in
sequence.

TODO: edit this description before publishing.

examples.title: SISI — Organic Sculpt
examples.description:
3-axis machining of a freeform organic surface. Uses adaptive clearing
for stock removal and parallel / scallop finishing for the curved
surfaces. Shows how Fusion 360 handles dense mesh geometry directly
inside a CAM workflow.

TODO: edit this description before publishing.

examples.title: Vacuum Fixture Plate
examples.description:
Vacuum fixture base plate with a regular grid of square pockets. The
pocket pattern is generated as a single pattern operation — one of the
simplest yet most useful jobs for any router shop that holds plastic
or thin metal sheets with vacuum.
